commit.message:
feat: interactive placeholder chips in test message dialog

When "Usar dados reais" is active, the dialog now shows:
1. Template with clickable placeholder chips ({nome}, {valor}, etc.)
2. Panel listing all placeholders with their real resolved values
3. Toggle to switch between template view and final message preview

Clicking a placeholder highlights it in both the template and the
values panel, making it clear what each variable resolves to.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: lower loss% for levels 2-3 to match perceptual expectation of "micro-pérdidas"

With -ec 0 (no error concealment) and -g 1 (all I-frames), each lost
packet creates a bright raw macroblock — visually dramatic even at low
rates. Level 2 at 0.1% produced 1 artifact every 2.5s, too frequent
for "micro-pérdidas". Level 2 now at 0.05% (1 every ~5s) and level 3
at 0.2% (~1/s), creating genuine progression from subtle to noticeable.

commit.message:
v0.7.0: differential fingerprint, bridged ring fix, whitepaper

- Add chirality_differential_fingerprint() using only diff_cos + diff_sin
  sub-vectors (16 raw dims -> 128). Enantiomer cosine: -0.09 to -0.93
  (vs full FP 0.72-0.98). Achiral norm = 0. Modes: pure_diff, chi_diff.
- Add _is_bridged_bicyclic() detection via SSSR ring overlap analysis.
  Bridged rings (norbornane, camphor, DXM) get dedicated fallback chain
  with MMFF at all stages for correct bridgehead geometry.
- Expose fingerprint + differential_fingerprint on ChiralityResult.
- 24 new tests (73 total in modified files), 296/302 full suite pass.
- 7-page whitepaper with actual benchmark numbers.
- Atlas v5 diff FP benchmark script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

embeddedbrowserwebview: Add stub DLL for Microsoft Edge WebView2

Add a minimal stub implementation of the embeddedbrowserwebview DLL
that provides the WebView2 runtime COM interfaces. The stub gracefully
returns E_NOTIMPL to the creation callback, preventing crashes in
applications that use WebView2 (e.g. enova365 for KSeF invoice rendering).

Includes webview2.idl with core COM interfaces, registry entries for
EdgeUpdate client discovery, and wine.inf entries to place the DLL in
the expected EdgeWebView application directory.

commit.message:
fix: Preserve numRows for zero-column RecordBatch in IPC (#402)

## Summary

When a zero-column RecordBatch is deserialized from IPC,
`ensureSameLengthData`
in the `RecordBatch` constructor recomputes length from children via
`chunks.reduce((max, col) => Math.max(max, col.length), 0)`. With zero
children,
this always returns 0 — discarding the original length from the IPC
message header.

Other Arrow implementations (PyArrow, Arrow Go, arrow-rs) correctly
preserve
numRows for zero-column batches.

## Fix

Pass `this.data.length` to `ensureSameLengthData` as the explicit
`maxLength`
parameter, which the function already accepts as an optional third
argument.
For batches with columns, `this.data.length` already matches the max
column
length, so there is no behavior change.

## Tests

- Read a PyArrow-generated zero-column IPC stream (100 rows) and verify
numRows
- JS round-trip: write + read zero-column batch, verify numRows
preserved
- Direct constructor: verify zero-column RecordBatch preserves length

Closes #401

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(users): fix scroll, add close button and metadata

### Why
User detail panel was not scrollable, making it impossible to reach Save/Delete buttons or activity tabs on smaller viewports.

commit.message:
fix(migrations): renumber Rashid's migrations to avoid number conflicts

Rashid's 0015/0016/0017 clashed with main's 0015_riskexception,
0016_riskexception_expired_status, 0017_riskregister_idx migrations.

Renamed to 0025/0026/0027 with correct dependency chain:
- 0025_add_system_setting     → depends on 0024_committee...
- 0026_ecc_domain_subdomain   → depends on 0025_add_system_setting
- 0027_add_compliance_control → depends on 0026_ecc_domain_subdomain

This creates the missing tables: system_settings, compliance_domains,
compliance_subdomains, compliance_controls — fixing the 500 error on
/compliance/frameworks (ecc_controls table did not exist).

fix: remove macOS constraint from list command (#30)

The list subcommands (questions, jurisdictions) only require Vapor,
Fluent, and FluentSQLiteDriver — all of which support Linux. The
#if os(macOS) guard was inherited from import/edit/save commands that
have genuine macOS dependencies, not from any requirement of list itself.

commit.message:
feat(web): intercept file path links in agent messages to open workspace viewer (#305) (#305)

Agent messages (e.g. from Codex) often contain file path links like
`omnimem/core.py:4318` which browsers resolve as broken server URLs.

Now these links are intercepted and open the file in the workspace panel:
- Delegated click handler on MessageItem detects file path hrefs
- WorkspacePanel auto-selects the sending agent, switches to files tab
- File is requested via WebSocket and displayed with syntax highlighting
- Target line is highlighted and scrolled into view

  EDT brand logo, responsive/mobile layout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: switch from build: to image: to eliminate Codespace rebuild delay

- devcontainer.json was using build: which causes Codespace to always
  rebuild from Dockerfile (~2-3 min npm install) — cacheFrom is ignored
- Switch all 5 CC labs to image: ghcr.io/sanjeev23oct/ailab-cc-base:latest
  (pulls pre-built image in ~15-30s instead of rebuilding)
- Rewrite prebuilds.yml to use docker/build-push-action to build
  labs/Dockerfile and push shared base image to ghcr.io on every change

IMPORTANT: Manually trigger the "Build Dev Container Base Image" workflow
in GitHub Actions before creating a new Codespace, to ensure the image exists.

commit.message:
Add .env.production with public client-side keys for Vercel builds

After untracking .env, Vercel builds lost the VITE_ variables needed
at build time. These are all PUBLIC keys (Supabase anon key, Stripe
publishable key) designed to be embedded in client-side JS — not secrets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Expand trail coverage to both mountains; reweight scoring per NSAA guidelines

Expanded bounding box to cover Lincoln Peak and Mt. Ellen (was only Lincoln Peak).
Updated composite score weights from 30/40/30 to 15/35/50 (vd/avg/max) based on
NSAA official guidelines which rate trails primarily on their steepest section,
with average sustained slope secondary and vertical drop not a direct criterion.

Remove default React favicon.ico

feat: add IssueView skin + GrassView UX improvements

IssueView (new skin):
- FetchIssues.ps1: fetches open issues & PRs from GitHub REST API
  per repo (reuses Repo1/2/3 from Settings.inc), up to 5 issues + 5 PRs
- Columns: type badge (I/PR) | #num | title | relative age
- Issue badge = GitHub green, PR badge = purple (theme-independent)
- Partial-failure guard: keeps existing INI if any repo fetch fails
- launcher_issues.vbs + run_issues.bat for background/manual execution

GrassView improvements:
- Today cell highlight: white border (StrokeWidth 1.5) on today's cell
- Relative date tooltips: Today / Yesterday / N days ago
- Streak counter: current streak + best streak, right-aligned on total row

Settings.inc.example: add Repo1/2/3 example entries

Scale diagrams to match KaTeX display math (×1.21)

next to web fonts at equal pixel size. Our tikzcd diagrams need the
same scaling to look balanced alongside $$ display math.

commit.message:
fix: corriger "Database error checking email" sur envoi invitation

Remplace le client Supabase direct (@supabase/supabase-js) par le client
SSR (@/lib/supabase/server) dans la route send-invitation, alignant le
pattern d'authentification sur celui de /api/admin/users qui fonctionne.

Le client direct avec header Authorization manuelle causait des erreurs
GoTrue "Database error checking email" lors de la vérification du token.
